name: Assetnote Wordlists
purpose: Auto-generated technology-specific wordlists mined monthly from Common Crawl data, grouped by detected framework
author: Shubham Shah (shubs / infosec_au)
why_notable: Australian bug bounty hunter Shubham Shah identified the gap in SecLists: generic web-path lists don't know what framework the target runs. Laravel's /horizon debug panel or Spring Boot's /actuator/heapdump don't appear in generic lists — you need framework-specific wordlists. Shah built a pipeline that processes monthly Common Crawl dumps (petabyte-scale public web crawls by Gil Elbaz's nonprofit), detects technology stacks via Wappalyzer signatures, groups URLs by detected framework, and frequency-ranks paths within each group. The result: /actuator appears at rank 5 in a Spring Boot list versus rank 50,000 in a generic list. Free wordlists at wordlists.assetnote.io are auto-regenerated monthly. The company was acquired by Netlify in 2022; free access was unaffected.
